name: PINATA Workbench
description: Reddit-powered product research workbench. Deliberate, model-in-the-loop: search Reddit, save approved signals, cluster pain themes, challenge your thesis, score opportunities, and publish a Winner / Next Up / Losers matrix.

tools.name: search_reddit
tools.description: Query Reddit posts or comments via PullPush. Returns pre-cleaned results sorted by a pain_score (upvotes + pain-phrase hits). Use this first — browse, don't commit.

query.description: Search string. For posts this matches the title; for comments it matches the body.

kind.description: What to search: 'posts' or 'comments'. Default: posts.

subreddit.description: Optional subreddit name without r/ prefix (e.g. 'SaaS', 'Entrepreneur').

sort_type.description: Sort order: 'num_comments' (most discussed), 'score' (most upvoted), 'created_utc' (newest). Default: num_comments.

min_score.description: Minimum Reddit upvote score. Filters out low-signal noise. Default: 5.

size.description: Number of results to return. Max 100. Default: 15.

tools.name: save_signal
tools.description: Persist a specific Reddit post or comment you have approved as a meaningful research signal. Deduplicates by ID. Add tags and a note to explain why it matters.

id.description: Reddit item ID — used for deduplication.

title.description: Post title or comment thread title.

body.description: The cleaned text body of the post or comment.

score.description: Reddit upvote score at time of saving.

tags.description: Comma-separated tags to categorize this signal, e.g. 'pain,pricing,workaround'.

note.description: Your research note explaining why this signal matters.

tools.name: list_signals
tools.description: List all saved signals in the current session, with optional tag filter. Use this to review what you've collected before clustering or scoring.

tag.description: Optional tag to filter by (e.g. 'pain', 'pricing'). Omit to list all.

tools.name: cluster_signals
tools.description: Group all saved signals into named pain-theme clusters. Groups by explicit tags first, then keyword co-occurrence for untagged signals. Results are persisted in the session and included in the final matrix.

tools.name: challenge_thesis
tools.description: Run adversarial Reddit searches to find disconfirming evidence for a stated opportunity. Looks for satisfied users, existing solutions, low WTP, and fragmented demand. Always run this before scoring.

thesis.description: The opportunity or product idea you want to stress-test. Be specific.

size.description: Number of results per adversarial query. Default: 10.

tools.name: score_opportunity
tools.description: Score one opportunity across the 7-dimension PINATA rubric. Each dimension is 1–5 and weighted to a 0–100 final score. Auto-assigns Winner (≥70), Next Up (45–69), or Loser (<45).

name.description: Short opportunity name, e.g. 'Reddit Pain Tracker SaaS'.

description.description: What the product or feature does, and who it's for.

pain_intensity.description: How severe is the pain? 1 (mild) to 5 (acute, blocking).

recurrence.description: How often does this pain come up? 1 (rarely) to 5 (constant).

willingness_to_pay.description: Do people signal budget or pay intent? 1 (none) to 5 (strong explicit signals).

dissatisfaction.description: How bad are current alternatives? 1 (mostly fine) to 5 (no good option exists).

reachability.description: Can you reach these users? 1 (scattered/unknown) to 5 (clear channel, subreddit, or community).

competitive_whitespace.description: How open is the competitive space? 1 (crowded) to 5 (clear gap).

build_complexity.description: How hard to build? Inverse scale — 5 means easy. 1 = very hard.

tools.name: publish_matrix
tools.description: Write the final opportunity matrix to disk as both JSON and Markdown. Includes all scored opportunities, signal clusters, and your narrative summaries. This is the deliverable.

winner.description: Narrative description of the winning opportunity and why it wins.

next_up.description: Description of next-up opportunities and what evidence is still needed.

losers.description: What was rejected and why — be specific about the weakness.

research_notes.description: Optional caveats, evidence gaps, or confidence notes.

output_path.description: Optional custom output path. Defaults to ~/.pinata/opportunity_matrix.json.
